     The Numbers Business Report

The Kite Runner (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,800,078Details
International Box Office $58,380,667
Worldwide Box Office $74,180,745
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,632,646
Total $7,632,646
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Spanning from the final days of Afghanistan’s monarchy to the atrocities of the Taliban reign, an epic tale of fathers and sons, of friendship and betrayal, an unlikely friendship develops between Amir, the son of a wealthy Afghan businessman, and Hassan, a servant to Amir and his father. During a kite-flying tournament, an unspeakable event changes the nature of their relationship forever. As an adult haunted by the childhood betrayal, Amir seeks redemption by returning to his war-torn native land to make peace with himself and reconcile his cowardice.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$471,713 (3.0% of total gross)
Legs:8.56 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:21.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:35 opening theaters/715 max. theaters, 5.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $26,785,628

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 14th, 2007 (Limited) by Paramount Vantage
Video Release: March 25th, 2008 by Dreamworks Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for strong thematic material including the sexual assault of a child, violence and brief strong language
Running Time: 125 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prologue
Geographical setting: Afghanistan
Plot point: Child Abuse, Non-Chronological, The Past Catching up with You
Social setting: Adopted Family
Subgenre: Life Drama
Time period setting: 1970s, 2000s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Participant Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Spanning from the final days of Afghanistan’s monarchy to the atrocities of the Taliban reign, an epic tale of fathers and sons, of friendship and betrayal, an unlikely friendship develops between Amir, the son of a wealthy Afghan businessman, and Hassan, a servant to Amir and his father. During a kite-flying tournament, an unspeakable event changes the nature of their relationship forever. As an adult haunted by the childhood betrayal, Amir seeks redemption by returning to his war-torn native land to make peace with himself and reconcile his cowardice.
